title: The One Where Ross Hugs Rachel

jokes.text: Want to hear something weird? Always. I didn't get the annulment.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's eager 'Always' response to hearing something weird creates expectation of normal gossip
jokes.punchline: Ross drops the bombshell that he's still married, completely subverting the casual tone
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection and economy. The casual delivery makes the reveal funnier.

jokes.text: What? We're still married. Don't tell Rachel. See you later.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's shocked reaction to the marriage revelation
jokes.punchline: Ross casually drops more bombs then tries to escape like nothing happened
jokes.explanation: Ross's awkward attempt to escape responsibility is perfectly in character and cringe-inducing.

jokes.text: There's a cab. Taxi! That's great. What timing! My God, huh? There you go. We're gonna walk. It's right at the Angelika. The Angelika! Go! Go!
jokes.setup: Ross desperately trying to get Rachel into a cab to avoid Phoebe
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top panic and contradictory instructions (cab then walking)
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy works but feels slightly forced and verbose.

jokes.text: Three divorces! Three divorces! Yes, in that high voice.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining what he heard at the lawyer's office
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately knows it's in a 'high voice' - the universal voice of judgment
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about how judgment sounds - relatable and well-delivered.

jokes.text: Don't worry about that. Your tombstone can say whatever you want. It can say, 'Ross Geller: Good at Marriage.'
jokes.setup: Ross worried about his tombstone saying 'Three Divorces'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe suggests the opposite ironic inscription
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark irony delivered with Phoebe's characteristic off-kilter helpfulness.

jokes.text: You know? Mine's gonna say, 'Phoebe Buffay: Buried Alive.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of tombstone inscriptions
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's completely random, dark prediction about her own death
jokes.explanation: Quintessentially Phoebe - absurd, dark, and completely unexpected. Only she could deliver this line.

jokes.text: Rachel is one of my closest friends. But being the only one that knows about this makes me feel special. Okay.
jokes.setup: Phoebe torn between loyalty to Rachel and keeping Ross's secret
jokes.punchline: She immediately abandons moral principles for the ego boost of having secret knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - morality vs. feeling special, with special winning instantly.

jokes.text: Okay, stop, stop! Phoebe? Hey, Rach! What was that? Sorry. Mix-up.
jokes.setup: Phoebe literally physically stopping Rachel from going somewhere
jokes.punchline: Her weak 'mix-up' excuse after obviously interfering
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy, functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You'd better hurry. The Angelika! Go!
jokes.setup: Previous scene with Ross frantically shouting about the Angelika
jokes.punchline: Phoebe now randomly shouting the same thing
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that works, though not deeply clever.

jokes.text: No, when it comes to sweets, he's surprisingly strict.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Joey not allowing a gumball machine
jokes.punchline: The idea that Joey, who eats everything, has strict sweet rules
jokes.explanation: Good character-based irony - Joey being strict about food is inherently funny.

jokes.text: No, I keep trying. And I can get out, 'Joey, I have to..' Then I lose my nerve and I finish with, 'go to the bathroom.'
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to tell Joey he's moving out
jokes.punchline: His cowardly retreat into bathroom excuses
jokes.explanation: Relatable cowardice humor with good specificity about the bathroom excuse.

jokes.text: He may think I'm sick.
jokes.setup: Previous line about constantly saying he needs to go to the bathroom
jokes.punchline: The logical consequence - Joey thinking he has health issues
jokes.explanation: Good logical follow-up, economical delivery.

jokes.text: That's how it'll work now? You'll just order me around? -Pretty much. -All right.
jokes.setup: Monica being decisive about telling their friends
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate acceptance of being bossed around
jokes.explanation: Classic relationship dynamic humor, well-executed with their characters.

jokes.text: We have something to tell you. Oh, my God! You're pregnant! No!
jokes.setup: Serious announcement setup
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately jumps to pregnancy, which Chandler emphatically denies
jokes.explanation: Predictable but functional misdirection joke.

jokes.text: I'm really happy for you guys. Congratulations. See you later.
jokes.setup: Joey processing the news that Chandler is moving out
jokes.punchline: His forced politeness followed by immediate exit
jokes.explanation: Good emotional beat showing Joey's hurt disguised as politeness.

jokes.text: I gotta go. I've got an acting job. Like you'd believe that.
jokes.setup: Joey making an excuse to leave
jokes.punchline: Immediately acknowledging his own lie about having work
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor that's perfectly Joey - his acting struggles become the punchline.

jokes.text: Maybe that's now.
jokes.setup: Chandler promising to move back when he and Monica break up
jokes.punchline: Joey's pointed suggestion that maybe they should break up now
jokes.explanation: Dark but economical - Joey's hurt expressed through destructive suggestion.

jokes.text: He's a terrible roommate. Terrible. He forgets to... He always, he always... Who am I kidding? He's the best roommate!
jokes.setup: Joey trying to badmouth Chandler to Monica
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to find fault, ending in honest praise
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - Joey's loyalty overcoming his hurt feelings.

jokes.text: Oh, look, here's an article about people who marry people and don't tell them. -Really? -Yeah, I'm reading Ross Geller Magazine.
jokes.setup: Phoebe claiming to find a relevant article
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's reading 'Ross Geller Magazine' - an impossible publication
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe absurdism - the idea of a magazine devoted to Ross's problems is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: You're pregnant. No! Chandler and I are moving in together.
jokes.setup: Monica announcing news to Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately guesses pregnancy again
jokes.explanation: Weak running gag - repetition without escalation or new angle.

jokes.text: But if you were pregnant, what would you name it? Hint, Phoebe.
jokes.setup: Monica denying pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to what they'd name a hypothetical baby, hinting it should be Phoebe
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's thoughtfulness toward Phoebe.

jokes.text: Oh, my little sister and my best friend... shacking up.
jokes.setup: Ross processing that Monica and Chandler are moving in together
jokes.punchline: The crude 'shacking up' description after the sweet setup
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between sweet and crude, fits Ross's awkwardness.

jokes.text: Big day, huh? People moving in. People getting annulled.
jokes.setup: Ross commenting on the day's events
jokes.punchline: Casually dropping 'annulled' like it's happening when it's not
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows he's lying.

jokes.text: If you see her, give her some really bad news, so mine doesn't seem so bad.
jokes.setup: Ross asking Phoebe to find Rachel
jokes.punchline: His selfish request to soften his own bad news with worse news
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven selfishness, feels authentically Ross.

jokes.text: My life is an embarrassment. I should just go live under somebody's stairs.
jokes.setup: Ross wallowing in self-pity
jokes.punchline: The specific, pathetic image of living under stairs
jokes.explanation: Good self-pitying specificity, though not exceptional.

jokes.text: The only guys below me will be Four-Divorce Guy... Murderer Guy... and geologists.
jokes.setup: Ross describing the bottom of the dating barrel
jokes.punchline: Escalating from four divorces to murder to... geologists
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect Ross prejudice - academic snobbery makes geologists worse than murderers in his mind.

jokes.text: Hi. My friend Ross is about to be divorced for the third time. But wouldn't you date him? And if you wait right here, I'll go get Ross.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to prove Ross is dateable
jokes.punchline: Her absolutely terrible approach of leading with his worst quality
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - Monica's well-meaning but completely counterproductive approach.

jokes.text: Listen, for the first four years of my work, everybody called me...
jokes.setup: Chandler about to commiserate with Rachel about name problems
jokes.punchline: He clearly stops himself from saying something embarrassing
jokes.explanation: Simple comedic beat - the joke is what he doesn't say.

jokes.text: By the end of the day, the mailroom guys were calling me Rocky.
jokes.setup: Rachel's name problems at work, being called Racquel
jokes.punchline: The progression from Racquel to Rocky by the mailroom
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how names evolve in workplace gossip.

jokes.text: Well, I still think you're very, very nice and very pretty. What? All yours, babe.
jokes.setup: Stranger making inappropriate comments to Rachel
jokes.punchline: Chandler's confused reaction and the guy treating Rachel like property
jokes.explanation: Awkward interaction that sets up dating theme but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: The three of us are gonna have such a good time living together! Yes, we are.
jokes.setup: Rachel misunderstanding that she's included in the living arrangement
jokes.punchline: Monica's pained agreement while knowing she'll have to break the news
jokes.explanation: Good setup for awkwardness - the painful misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Chandler, you'll have to watch those long showers you take... because you know Racquel can't be late.
jokes.setup: Rachel giving advice for their living situation
jokes.punchline: Using her own work nickname from earlier in the episode
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows her embracing the nickname problem.

jokes.text: Come and knock on our door / We'll be waiting for you
jokes.setup: Rachel is clearly singing while doing something domestic
jokes.punchline: She's singing the Three's Company theme, referencing the three-person living situation
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that parallels the living situation setup.

jokes.text: First marriage: Wife's hidden sexuality. Not my fault. Second marriage: Said the wrong name at the altar. A little my fault. Third marriage: They shouldn't let you get married when you're drunk and have writing on your face. Nevada's fault.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his divorce history to strangers
jokes.punchline: His blame assignment getting more ridiculous, ending with blaming Nevada itself
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect Ross rationalization - the blame shifting and 'writing on your face' detail are gold.

jokes.text: The divorces don't bother me. I'd date him. But not while he's still married.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to prove he's dateable despite divorces
jokes.punchline: Woman would date him except for the secret marriage he's hiding
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - the audience knows about the secret marriage.

jokes.text: It's just that he's obviously still in love with this Rachel girl.
jokes.setup: Woman explaining why she wouldn't date Ross
jokes.punchline: An outsider immediately seeing what Ross denies
jokes.explanation: Good plot advancement disguised as a joke - outsider perspective revealing truth.

jokes.text: And I have loved her in the past... but now, she is just my wife.
jokes.setup: Ross denying current feelings for Rachel
jokes.punchline: The casual 'just my wife' while trying to downplay the relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross logic - minimizing marriage while trying to sound casual about it.

jokes.text: We were drunk! I would've married Joey with that amount of alcohol. You could do a lot worse than Joey Tribbiani!
jokes.setup: Ross explaining the drunken marriage
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately defending Joey's marriage potential
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Phoebe's loyalty to Joey overriding the point Ross was making.

jokes.text: Now he's using this three-divorces reason... because he wants to stay married to her because he loves her. I must say, well done. Bravo, Meg.
jokes.setup: Meg analyzing Ross's psychology
jokes.punchline: Phoebe treating it like a performance, applauding the analysis
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe moment - treating real psychology like entertainment.

jokes.text: She means nothing to me. Nothing! Okay, now I wouldn't date you because you seem a little creepy.
jokes.setup: Ross overcompensating to prove he doesn't love Rachel
jokes.punchline: His intensity backfiring completely
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ross's self-sabotage when he tries too hard.

jokes.text: I am so attracted to him right now.
jokes.setup: Ross acting creepy about Rachel
jokes.punchline: Phoebe finding the creepiness attractive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - finding attraction in completely inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: You just said.. Of course you're my best friend.
jokes.setup: Chandler catching Monica in contradictory statements about friendship
jokes.punchline: Her immediate backpedaling
jokes.explanation: Functional relationship humor but nothing special.

jokes.text: Are you pregnant? No, but I'm throwing this shirt away.
jokes.setup: Monica deflecting Rachel's pregnancy guess
jokes.punchline: Random shirt comment as deflection
jokes.explanation: Weak deflection humor, feels forced.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. That's funny. I can't believe I did that.
jokes.setup: Rachel realizing her misunderstanding about the living situation
jokes.punchline: Her embarrassed reaction to her own assumption
jokes.explanation: Good embarrassment beat, relatable but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Oh, I guess it really is concentrated.
jokes.setup: Discovering dishwashing soap that was there when they moved in
jokes.punchline: Deadpan realization that old soap must be very concentrated to last so long
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan observation, economical and fits Chandler's style.

jokes.text: No more Joey and Chan's. No more J and C's. Want to go to Joey and Chandler's? Can't. It's not there.
jokes.setup: Joey mourning the loss of their apartment identity
jokes.punchline: His increasingly dramatic examples of what won't exist anymore
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Joey's emotional processing through practical examples.

jokes.text: Don't you remember that night, it was late and I was feeling sick... and you said I would feel better tomorrow?
jokes.setup: Joey claiming they had deep talks
jokes.punchline: His example is the most basic possible interaction
jokes.explanation: Great deflation - Joey's idea of a deep conversation is hilariously mundane.

jokes.text: What the hell is that? That's disgusting.
jokes.setup: They clearly see something gross while going through possessions
jokes.punchline: Their disgusted reactions to some unidentified object
jokes.explanation: Basic visual gag, relies on gross-out humor without cleverness.

jokes.text: And you're upset because you didn't make your best friend cry?
jokes.setup: Monica complaining Rachel didn't cry about the living situation
jokes.punchline: Chandler pointing out how twisted that sounds
jokes.explanation: Good observation about Monica's selfish disappointment in not getting an emotional reaction.

jokes.text: Okay, I gotta ask, who calls us that?
jokes.setup: Joey referring to them as 'J-Man and Channy's'
jokes.punchline: Chandler questioning who actually uses these nicknames
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about sitcom naming conventions and who actually uses cute nicknames.

jokes.text: Yes. Second in two years. Third, overall.
jokes.setup: Lawyer asking about Ross's second marriage in two years
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact correction about the total count
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of embarrassing information with casual precision.

jokes.text: I have been a divorce attorney for 23 years... and never have I had so much business from one client.
jokes.setup: Lawyer's professional experience
jokes.punchline: Ross being an unprecedented repeat customer
jokes.explanation: Good professional perspective on Ross's unusual situation.

jokes.text: Have you considered therapy? I think just the annulment for today.
jokes.setup: Lawyer suggesting therapy for Ross's pattern
jokes.punchline: Ross treating therapy like a menu option he's not ordering today
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan treatment of therapy as a casual service option.

jokes.text: What about someone who looks like Rachel? I will think about the therapy.
jokes.setup: Ross learning he needs Rachel present for the annulment
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous suggestion followed by reluctant therapy consideration
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ross's desperation leading to absurd solutions.

jokes.text: What is that? I don't know.
jokes.setup: They're dividing possessions
jokes.punchline: Finding something neither recognizes as belonging to either of them
jokes.explanation: Simple situational humor about mysterious belongings.

jokes.text: An era is defined as a significant period of time. It was significant for me. Maybe it wasn't significant to you!
jokes.setup: Rachel questioning if six years counts as an era
jokes.punchline: Monica getting defensive and making it personal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's need for validation and tendency to take things personally.

jokes.text: It's just a little hard to believe. What's hard to believe? I mean, it's you guys. You do this kind of stuff, you know? You were gonna get married in Vegas, and then you backed out.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why she's not upset
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact assessment of Monica and Chandler's track record
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about their tendency to make grand gestures they don't follow through on.

jokes.text: Really? Yeah, sweetie. You mean, we're not gonna live together anymore? Oh, my God. I'm gonna miss you so much.
jokes.setup: Rachel finally understanding the reality of the situation
jokes.punchline: Her delayed but genuine emotional reaction
jokes.explanation: Emotional payoff that resolves Monica's frustration, though not particularly comedic.

jokes.text: So did you get the annulment? I couldn't. I knew it. Because you love Rachel.
jokes.setup: Ross returning from the lawyer
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately jumping to the love conclusion again
jokes.explanation: Running gag that's become predictable, though fits Phoebe's character.

jokes.text: I've never been more convinced of your love for her.
jokes.setup: Ross insisting he'll tell Rachel to get the annulment
jokes.punchline: Phoebe finding his protests more convincing evidence of love
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe logic - the more Ross denies it, the more convinced she becomes.

jokes.text: So Ross and Rachel got married. Monica and Chandler almost got married. You think we should hook up? Oh, we do. But not just yet.
jokes.setup: Joey observing the marriage pattern among friends
jokes.punchline: Phoebe matter-of-factly agreeing they'll hook up eventually
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for the extended fantasy sequence, perfectly in character for both.

jokes.text: Well, first Chandler and Monica will get married... and be filthy rich, by the way. But it won't work out.
jokes.setup: Phoebe laying out their future romantic timeline
jokes.punchline: Casual prediction of Chandler's wealth and marriage failure
jokes.explanation: Great escalation beginning - Phoebe's casual predictions become increasingly specific and absurd.

jokes.text: Then I'll marry Chandler for the money. And you'll marry Rachel and have the beautiful kids. Great. But then we ditch them and we get married.
jokes.setup: Continuing the romantic timeline
jokes.punchline: The mercenary marriage plan followed by abandonment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - the casual planning of multiple betrayals delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: We'll have Chandler's money and Rachel's kids. Getting custody will be easy because of Rachel's drinking problem.
jokes.setup: The marriage scheme continuing
jokes.punchline: Inventing Rachel's drinking problem as a custody advantage
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist escalation - casually inventing character flaws for strategic advantage is quintessentially Phoebe.

jokes.text: What about Ross? I don't want to go into the whole thing. But we have words and I kill him.
jokes.setup: Joey asking about Ross in their future plan
jokes.punchline: Phoebe casually mentioning murdering Ross after an argument
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination - the casual dismissal of murder as a minor detail is peak Phoebe absurdism. The economy of 'I don't want to go into the whole thing' makes it even funnier.
